root: ^The path you've been walking along seems to have taken you to a swamp.
^Now though, you're a bit unsure of how to proceed, as there's a suspicious lump in the puddle ahead of you.
^All sorts of wild theories start running in your head - many dangerous beasts call swamps their home.
^It could be a giant frog, a troll, or even something like a young hydra!
^As you stand there, lost in your thought trying to figure out what it is, the lump rises from the puddle!
^? This isn't the best place to just stand around you know."
^You jump a little as you're pulled back to reality. The creature spoke to you?
^"I uh. I'm alright, I think? Unless you're about to change that."
^"No no, I'm not the type to bully
^Phew. This... creature? Seems friendly at least. Although...

b: ^Right, it's a kappa. You recall having seen one of these before, maybe even this one!
^"Mmm, not that I remember at least. Might have been the previous occupant of this swamp?"
^"We do winter in the mountains, and settling back down is a kind of 'first-come-first-serve' thing"
^"Anyhow, since ye've met our kind before, ya know how this goes!"
^"So uh. Might be a rude question but... what exactly are you? I can't recall having seen one of your kind before."
^A kappa, huh. You wait a few moments for it to elaborate, yet it never does. Alright then.

root: ^"Ya got any goodies for me? I'll return the favor if ya do!"
^You wonder what it considers "goodies", and if there might be any other way to gain its favor...

s: ^Just for a moment you consider that this may come off as teasing, but you eat your apple in front of the kappa.
^You consider casting a bonfire on yourself, but the environment seems too moist.
^You talk to yourself for a moment, verbally pointing out details of the surrounding swamp.
^You consider your lockpick for a moment. Surely some day you'll get an opportunity to use it?
^The staff feels comforting in your hand. Perhaps you should just start walking with it in hand?
^You give your dagger a twirl. Sadly there's no desk around, so instead of five-finger-fillet, you just spin it around a little.
^You spin the frying pan in your hand once, then twice. What if you..?
^You quickly chug the potion and wait for the effects to kick in.
^"I'm just such a failure, I can't even call myself a wizard without a proper spellbook anymore..."
^You consider tying yourself up for a moment, but that'd hardly do you any good right now.
^Punching yourself just feels so... Uninnovative. So basic. So you decide to pass.
^Though a wet environment swamp seems like a good place to solve problems with electricity, you're unable to shock yourself.
^You consider the evil bottle in your hand. Not exactly suitable for drinking.
^You think about having some soup, but before you can crack the can open, the kappa speaks up!
^You manifest a snowball in your hand. But you have no idea what to do with it. So you chomp down on it.
^A cloud of smoke rapidly expands around you, and you bolt away from the swamp!
^Not wanting to deal with any of this, you start running off!
^You take a look at the stick. You don't have any ideas on how to use it on yourself though.
^Lying to yourself seems a tad impossible, so you give up on that for now.
^You recite a healing spell, and you're quickly surrounded by little wisps of energy!
^You consider the longsword in your hands. You can't really think of a way to use it on yourself.
^"You know, all things considered, I'm doing pretty great! I'm capable of anything, and I'm proud of myself.
^You consider hypnotizing yourself, but that could lead to trouble in this environment.
^You know better than to try to use telekinesis on yourself. You've heard the horror-stories after all.
^You take a long look at the rusty spear in your hand. You can't really think of a way to use it on yourself.
^You toss the kappa an apple. Can't go wrong with an apple, right?
^You don't feel like playing along with this thing, so you've chosen to roast it instead.
^"So, how's life in the swamp? Oh, and what do kappas eat
^You hold the lockpick toward the kappa. Perhaps it'll count as a "goodie"?
^You heft the staff in your hand. Time to show this thing what for!
^You manifest the dagger into your hand, and throw it at the kappa with all you've got!
^You give the frying pan a few spins. Time to show this thing the might of your dear frying pan!
^You hold the healing potion toward the kappa. Surely something like this will be a suitable "goodie"?
^"Are you really so broke that you have to sit here and ask for handouts? That's really embarrassing, could NOT be me."
^You hold the rope towards the kappa. You wonder if this'll count as a "goodie".
^You form a fist with your hand. Much easier than forming a plan, you think.
^You start building static electricity, and slowly moving towards the kappa. Time to give this thing what it deserves!
^You hold the bottle of poison towards the kappa. Now, should you lie, or tell the truth...
^Surely a can of soup will count as a "goodie"! You gently toss the can towards the kappa.
^You feel hesitant to even try this, but you eventually manifest a snowball and gently toss it at the kappa. Maybe it'll count..?
^You quickly pop a cloud of smoke at the kappa, and start running off!
^You quickly take off, and run past the kappa! You're not dealing with this thing!
^You look at the stick in your hands. Maybe the kappa can appreciate a cool stick?
^"I don't really have any goodies on me right now, but I promise to bring you some superb goodies really soon, if you give me something useful! Promise!"
^You hope the kappa needs some healing, and you especially hope that it counts healing as a "goodie". Time to give it a shot!
^You manifest the longsword in your hand. Time to show this thing your might!
^"You've got such a cozy looking home here! And your fur looks really soft and glossy!"
^You decide to hypnotize this thing, and start with casting the spell. Surely it'll just give you whatever it has if you have it hypnotized, right?
^You don't know how well it'll go, but you attempt to lift the kappa out of its puddle with your mind!
^The spear pops into your hand, and you ready your stance for a few moments, before dashing right at the creature!

c-0: ^The kappa stares you as you munch on the apple, its tongue plopping out ever-so-slightly. It's drooling.
^You finish the apple after a few moments, and the kappa seems to shake off its food-induced trance.

b: ^You were already feeling pretty healthy, so the apple didn't heal you this time.
^You feel the apple heal you slightly. Nice!

c-0: ^The kappa looks like it's almost pouting slightly. You almost feel a little bad now.
c-1: ^You give up this time, determined to light yourself on fire once you have a better opportunity.
c-2: ^The kappa seems very proud of itself. Apparently your rambling sounded like compliments to it?
c-4: ^Something seems to disagree, as the staff returns to being a card due to not being used. Damn.
c-5: ^The kappa looks highly uninterested in your antics, even as you flick the dagger in the air and catch it.

b: ^You've thought this through, and you're going to do it.
^You give it one more courtesy spin, and ready yourself.
^There's just no feeling quite like this...
^No, not now. No matter how nice it would feel to strike its cool cast-iron form against your skull, you can't.
^You don't have to wait long, and you quickly regain a good amount of health.
^You wait for a moment before figuring that you're already in perfect health. Eh, whatever.
^You don't have to wait long, and you quickly regain some health.

c-7: ^The kappa stares at you blankly. You wonder what it's thinking.
c-8: ^Disinterest written all over the kappa's face as it stares at you.
^...At least you think it's staring at you. Hard to say when you can't see its eyes.
^A few moments pass, and the awkwardness builds. You cough to break the silence.
c-9: ^The kappa seems interested in the rope. Could this rope perhaps pass for a "goodie"?
c-11: ^The kappa seems mildly amused at your attempts, at least.
c-12: ^Not that something being a stupid idea has stopped you before. Maybe..?

b: ^Maybe not. Host bodies are precious in this economy after all.
^You've thought it through, and the answer is yes. Somehow.
^You take a swig from the bottle, and your guts instantly start to churn.
^You keel over, as the poison burns through your innards, the pain making you curl up.
^You can't speak like this, so you just groan in response.
^"I uh. I'll just give ya a moment, 'kay?"
^The kappa withdraws back below the surface.
^Perhaps it'll keep away any threats while you recover at least. Right?
^After what feels like days, you finally feel fine enough to continue dealing with the kappa.
^"Hey uh, I think I'm good enough to continue our... whatever this is."
^The kappa quickly pops back up from under the water and looks at you expectantly.
^Yeah, maybe not. This stuff would burn through your guts like a hot knife through butter.

c-13: ^"Is that fer me? Looks like a proper goodie!"
^...Right. The kappa might take it as a grave offense, if you dared to eat the soup yourself.
c-14: ^Tastes like... cold. And not much else. Eh.
c-15: ^Screw all that, you're not giving the kappa anything!
^The kappa is quickly left behind, coughing as you make your exit.
c-16: ^The kappa seems uninterested. Apparently leaving was a valid option all along?

b: ^As the healing wisps make their way around your body, you feel the full healing effects kick in!
^As the healing wisps make their way around your body, you realize that you're already in perfect health. Eh, whatever.
^As the healing wisps make their way around your body, you quickly regain some health.

c-21: ^The kappa gives you a look. Hard to say what kind of a look.
^! It's important to know yer worth. Now, got any goodies fer me?"
c-23: ^Phew. That felt really cathartic. Although the kappa seems incredibly confused by your spontaneous outburst.
^"Yeah, perfectly ok! Just had to let that out. Apologies."
^"Gotcha. Could ya warn me next time?"
^You reassure the kappa that you'll give it a warning before yelling out again.
c-26: ^It attempts to catch the apple, but its reflexes are far too slow, and the apple plops into the water.
^The kappa fetches the apple from the water, and "cleans" it against its messy... fur?
^It tosses the entire apple in its mouth, and the crunch almost makes you jealous for a swamp apple. Almost.
c-27: ^You summon the bonfire right where the kappa is, but even before the first sparks form, it dives in its puddle!
^...Seems like fire doesn't do much to creatures submerged in water.
^You wait a few moments for the kappa the re-emerge, then a few more moments.
^It's been a while now. Seems like the kappa is done dealing with you.
c-28: ^"Swamp-life's pretty chill for the most part! None of the more dangerous creatures don't bother me either."
^"As fer what we eat... My favorites are apples, <size=60%>souls</size>, and cucumbers of course. I also eat fish sometimes."
^Wait, what was that second favorite food again? Perhaps it's better not to ask?
c-29: ^"Would this be counted as a goodie? It's a lockpick."
^, but I don't think I can use that here in the swamp, especially with these clumsy paws."
^Unused, the lockpick returns to card form. Worth a shot, you suppose.
c-31: ^Before the dagger is half-way toward the kappa, it's already below the water!
^The dagger flies past the only part of the kappa that's still on the surface, then sinks into the swamp. Damnit.
^You wait a few moments for the kappa to re-emerge, then a few more moments. After a while it becomes clear that it's staying down there.
c-33: ^"Would a healing potion be a suitable goodie?"
^"Sounds like a goodie alright! Don't toss it at me though, I'm terrible at catching anything."
^Good timing, since you were just about to toss the bottle to the kappa.
^You walk over, and hand the potion to the kappa.
c-34: ^...Perhaps that was a tad too far. You are pretty broke yourself...
^"That's real rude ya know. And I'm trying to trade with ya, not just get yer stuff fer free!"
^You're about to cuss out the creature, but it quickly dips back below the surface.
c-35: ^"Would you consider a rope to be a goodie? Could come in handy out here!"
^"Hmm. Hmmmmmm... I suppose yer right, it could be useful. I'll accept it.
c-37: ^Once you're charged up and close enough, you leap the rest of the way at the kappa, and give it a devastating shock!
^After taking a tiny moment to recover, it quickly dips below the surface, hiding from you.
^You wait a good few moments for it to reappear, but it seems to be done with you.
c-38: ^? Seems mighty dangerous by the look of the bottle!"
^Good thing you didn't immediately go with a lie.
^"It's a bottle of poison, figured it might come in handy out here. Do you think it's a good enough goodie?"
^"Hmm. Hmmmmmm... Don't think I'll have use fer it, but I could maybe trade it fer something else. I'll accept it."
c-39: ^The kappa attempts to catch the can, but its reflexes are far too slow. The can plops into the nearby water, and slowly floats back up.
^The kappa fetches the can, and studies it for a moment.
^"Soup, huh? Might be a bit hard on my beak to open, but it seems like a right proper goodie to me! Ya pass!"
c-40: ^The kappa attempts to catch the snowball, but its reflexes are far too slow. The snowball plops into the water and quickly dissolves.
^"Uh, yer goodie seems to have melted. Sorry about that,
^. I feel bad fer ya, so I'll give ya a consolation gift."
c-41: ^You hear the kappa withdraw back in the puddle, and you easily make your exit. Nice!
c-42: ^The kappa seems uninterested. Apparently leaving was a valid option all along?
c-43: ^"Would you perhaps count this one-of-a-kind cool stick as a goodie? It's even shaped like a wizard's staff!"
^. I'm sure ya consider sticks cool, but I can find those anywhere 'round here. No deal, sorry.
c-44: ^"Hmm. Hmmmmmm... No, no deal. I only deal in the here-and-now kind of goodies. Potential future goodies are not good enough."
c-45: ^"Say, you wouldn't happen to need any healing? I don't really have better goodies to offer you."
^"Well, now that ya mention it... I've been toughing it out, but I've got a splinter in my paw. If ya help me out with it, I'll give ya something in return. Deal?"
^You walk over and tell it to show you the splinter. It's pretty obvious, and you could easily pull it out with just your fingers.
^"I'll cast a healing spell before I yank it out so it doesn't hurt, okay?"
^It gives you a nod, and you quickly recite the spell, healing wisps surrounding the kappa.
^You quickly yank out the splinter as the wisps circle the kappa, and the tiny wound closes up almost immediately. Nice!
^! Totally worth some goodies from me!"
c-46: ^You bolt at the creature, and it immediately withdraws back into its puddle!
^You slice at it with all you've got!
^You wait a few moments for the kappa to re-emerge, then a few more moments. After a while it becomes clear that it's staying down there.
c-47: ^, I put great care into my home, and even more into my fur!"
^"I placed all the plants myself, and I think it really adds to the charm!"
^You wonder if it's talking about the plants on its fur, or the ones around its puddle. Perhaps both?
c-48: ^As the hypnosis finishes taking effect, the kappa gets a blank look on its face. Success!
^"Dear kappa, could you give me your treasures?"
c-49: ^You manage to get the kappa mostly out of the water, but the further up it gets, the harder it becomes as the lift from the water is lessened.
^After it's a bit more than half way up, it quickly splashes back down, as your mind buckles under the heavy load!
^After the splash has settled, the kappa is already fully withdrawn back to its hiding position. Damn.
^You wait a moment, then another moment, as it becomes clearer and clearer that it plans to stay down there.
c-50: ^Quickly after you start running, it withdraws back into its puddle!
^Not wanting to waste the spear, you drive it right at the part left on the surface!
^The spear *cracks* in two, negating most of the damage! The kappa doesn't react.
^You wait a few moments for the kappa to re-emerge, then a few more moments. After a while it becomes clear that it's staying down there.

KAPPA_BONK_PARAGRAPH: ^You bolt at the creature, and it immediately withdraws back into its puddle!
^You give it a *whack* with all you've got!
^You wait a few moments for the kappa to re-emerge, then a few more moments. After a while it becomes clear that it's staying down there.

b: ^"Now, seems like ya can make use of these cards I found. I've found a few, and since I can't seem to do anything with 'em, ya can have 'em!"
^Now that definitely sounds worth your trouble!
^As you're mentally celebrating, the kappa dives down into its puddle.
^You wait a few moments, and just as you start to think that you've been had, the kappa pops back up!
^"Sorry fer taking a while, it's tough to grab these tiny things with my paws. Now, have a look,
^After browsing through the cards, you thank the kappa.
^After thanking you, the kappa dips back into its puddle. Seems your business is concluded?
^You dust yourself off, and ready yourself for more adventuring!
^"Now, seems like ya can make use of these cards I found. I'm gonna show ya a few, and ya can pick yer favorite out of 'em. Deal?"
^The kappa dives down into its puddle.
^You wait a few moments, and just as you start to think that you've been had, the kappa pops back up!
^"Sorry fer taking a while, it's tough to grab these tiny things with my paws. Now, take yer pick,
^After making your choice, you thank the kappa.
^After thanking you, the kappa dips back into its puddle. Seems your business is concluded?
^You dust yourself off, and ready yourself for more adventuring!
^You've made your choice, so you keep running.
^You slow down after a while to spare your stamina, and perhaps because you feel like you missed out on something.
^Well. Nothing left to do here, since the kappa seems determined to stay withdrawn like that in its puddle.
^You toss a rock at its puddle as a parting shot, which makes a satisfying *plop* sound. That'll show it.
^You decide to keep adventuring, dragging your feet slightly, since you feel like you missed out on something.
